name: Deity Chalisa
title: Deity Chalisa | Forty Verses with Meaning
subtitle: Powerful forty-verse hymn
description: The Deity Chalisa is a powerful forty-verse devotional hymn composed in praise of the deity. Each verse extols the divine virtues, powers, and grace (100-500 characters).
significance: Explain the spiritual power of this 40-verse hymn and the blessings received by reciting it.

seo.title: Deity Chalisa Lyrics in Hindi | 40 Verses with Meaning
seo.description: Complete Deity Chalisa with all 40 verses in Hindi, English translation, and meaning. Learn this powerful devotional hymn.

sections.title: Doha (Invocation)
sections.hindi:
श्रीगुरु चरन सरोज रज, निज मनु मुकुरु सुधारि।
बरनऊं रघुबर बिमल जसु, जो दायक फल चारि॥

बुद्धिहीन तनु जानिके, सुमिरौं पवन-कुमार।
बल बुद्धि विद्या देहु मोहिं, हरहु कलेस बिकार॥
sections.english:
Having polished the mirror of my heart with the dust of my Guru's lotus feet, I sing the pure fame of the best of Raghu dynasty, which bestows the four fruits of life.

Knowing my body to be devoid of intelligence, I remember the Son of Wind (Hanuman). Grant me strength, intelligence, and knowledge, and remove all my afflictions and sorrows.
sections.meaning: The opening verses invoke the guru's blessings and dedicate the Chalisa to the deity, seeking divine gifts and removal of ignorance.

sections.title: Chaupai 1-10
sections.hindi:
जय हनुमान ज्ञान गुन सागर।
जय कपीस तिहुं लोक उजागर॥

राम दूत अतुलित बल धामा।
अंजनि-पुत्र पवनसुत नामा॥

[Continue verses 1-10...]
sections.english:
Victory to Hanuman, ocean of wisdom and virtue.
Victory to the Lord of Monkeys, illuminator of the three worlds.

Messenger of Rama, repository of immeasurable strength.
Son of Anjana, known as the son of Wind.

[Continue translations...]
sections.meaning: These verses describe the deity's divine qualities, attributes, and role in the cosmic order.

sections.title: Final Doha
sections.hindi:
पवनतनय संकट हरन, मंगल मूरति रूप।
राम लखन सीता सहित, हृदय बसहु सुर भूप॥
sections.english:
Son of Wind, remover of difficulties, embodiment of auspiciousness.
With Rama, Lakshmana, and Sita, reside in my heart, O Lord of Gods.
sections.meaning: The concluding verse seeks the deity's permanent residence in the devotee's heart along with Lord Rama, Lakshmana, and Mother Sita.

sections.title: Composer and Origin
sections.content: Information about who composed this Chalisa, when and where it was written, and its historical significance in Hindu devotional literature.

items.description: Removes negative energies and provides spiritual protection
Enhances memory, concentration, and mental strength
Removes diseases and provides physical vitality
Removes obstacles and brings success in all endeavors

sections.title: How to Recite the Chalisa
sections.content: Complete guidelines for proper recitation of the Chalisa including preparation, method, and concluding practices.
steps: Wake up early and purify yourself with bath
Sit facing east or north on an asana
Light a diya and incense before the deity's image
Begin with 'Om' chanting to focus the mind
Continue through all 40 Chaupais with concentration
Sit in meditation for a few minutes
sections.bestTime: Early morning (Brahma Muhurta) or evening after sunset

videos.title: Complete Chalisa with Lyrics
videos.description: Full 40 verses with on-screen Hindi lyrics
